entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Validated `source_dir` authored publishing support with passing unit tests and a successful builder run; the `auto-games` diff stays within the scoped builder/tests/fixture paths and includes no live manifest rollout/output regeneration changes.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Added `source_dir` publishing support to the arcade builder and test coverage for authored game directories, including static assets and missing-directory validation. Resolved the scope-drift quickfix by keeping the task infra-only, reverting live manifest/output changes, and aligning the task docs with that non-rollout scope. Refactor scan found no additional evidence-backed improvement.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Verified the authored `source_dir` contract with evidence: `auto-games` unittests passed and `scripts/build_arcade.py` ran successfully. Flagged a scope mismatch: current changes include a Corebound manifest/version bump and regenerated output files outside the task’s stated file-touch / “no release copy changes” constraints.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Re-ran the Builder flow against the active authored `source_dir` publishing task and confirmed the existing `auto-games` implementation satisfies the prompt without further code changes. Refactor was a no-op because the required unittest suite and build command both passed and did not expose an evidence-backed improvement within scope.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Classified the block as a stale quickfix / incorrect-block condition, not a product-code failure. The active `auto-games` task verification passes, and the only OPEN blocker was a demand to clean unrelated pre-existing workspace edits outside the task.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Ran verification for `source_dir` authored publishing. `auto-games` unittests passed and `scripts/build_arcade.py` produced authored output, but QA remains BLOCKED because `turnloop/` has unrelated working-tree changes (quickfix item 1) that violate scope/auditability.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Verified the authored `source_dir` publishing work in `auto-games`, updated the authored Corebound page to match manifest version `0.0.2`, and staged the authored test fixture files for reproducible coverage. The run remains blocked because `agents/work/quickfix.md` still has an unresolved OPEN scope-drift item for unrelated pre-existing `turnloop/` changes outside this task.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Ran QA against expectations for `source_dir` authored publishing. `auto-games` unit tests and `scripts/build_arcade.py` passed, but gaps remain: unrelated `turnloop/` changes in the working tree, untracked `auto-games/tests/fixtures/` content, and a Corebound version-display mismatch after bumping to `0.0.2`.

entries.title: Allow authored Corebound publishing
entries.summary: Added `source_dir` publishing to the arcade builder, covered the new manifest contract with authored-source tests and fixtures, and pointed the live Corebound manifest entry at the authored game directory with a patch version bump. Refactor pass found no evidence-backed follow-up changes to apply. Quickfix remained a no-op because `agents/work/quickfix.md` has no OPEN items.

entries.title: Decomposed Corebound playable mining loop
entries.summary: Decomposed the Corebound playable mining loop spec into four task cards covering authored build support, core gameplay loop, upgrades/persistence/onboarding, and manifest release updates, then moved the spec into the specs queue.

entries.title: Corebound First Playable Mining Loop
entries.summary: Researched the oldest inbox prompt and produced a greenfield spec for turning Corebound from a generated release page into its first playable mining build. The spec centers on one cohesive mining-extraction-upgrade loop and includes the minimal shared publishing-flow change needed to ship authored gameplay safely.
